scenarios.description: Expired/revoked OPENAI_API_KEY — error handler omits suggested_fix on synthetic CRITICAL finding, producing schema-invalid output that permanently blocks convergence
mode=code with empty git diff (Stage 3 before any code) silently produces status=ok approve, causing false convergence
OpenAI response truncated (finish_reason equivalent) but syntactically valid JSON — accepted as complete review, blocking findings silently dropped
OpenAI returns valid JSON with uppercase severity CRITICAL — case-sensitive convergence comparison misses it, false convergence reached
mode=plan with absent active-feature marker — double-slash path reads wrong specs/README.md instead of exiting 2
Output parent directory does not exist — undefined exit code, no sidecar written, misleading reviewer_json_missing CRITICAL injected by convergence
reviewer field in written JSON is model name not role name — key mismatch with expected_roles causes permanent reviewer_json_missing CRITICAL
Non-integer --iteration value produces invalid JSON or Python traceback with undefined exit code
active-feature marker contains spaces — bash word-splitting truncates path, correct README silently skipped
No test planned for schema enum mismatch — silent false-convergence failure mode has no regression guard
Bash(OPENAI_API_KEY=:*) permission entry auto-approves any command prefixed with OPENAI_API_KEY= — prompt injection can execute arbitrary commands
Unvalidated .tlmforge_active_feature content allows path traversal — '../../../.ssh/id_rsa' exfiltrated to api.openai.com via mode=plan
API error message string-interpolated into JSON — error text containing double-quote breaks JSON boundary, blocking convergence
client.chat.completions.create() deprecated for gpt-5.5 — must use Responses API. Wrong API causes tool-calling failures.
Python script has no atomic-write requirement — interrupted write leaves absent or partial output file, triggering false reviewer_json_missing CRITICAL
